summary: Backend Node.js/Express/Prisma smoke test after migration from Python. 5/5 logins OK, /auth/me OK, queue ordered correctly (urgente>preferencial>normal), LGPD hides history when not accepted, prescriptions (create/409 duplicate/justification override) OK with GOVBR- validation_code, users role filter OK (2 medicos), vacancies/active returns 3 with remaining_seconds, secretario dashboard has all required KPIs + weekly_trend (7d), refs/cid|tuss|sigtap return results, 403 authz enforced correctly (medico->dashboard, atendente->prescriptions). CRITICAL BUG: Node process CRASHES with unhandled PrismaClientValidationError on POST /api/exams and POST /api/users (admin) with reasonable payloads, and on POST /api/prescriptions if 'duration' is sent instead of 'duration_days'. Supervisor auto-restarts but concurrent requests get 502. Also audit-logs returns 0 entries despite many audited actions.
passed: POST /api/auth/login for all 5 credentials (admin, medico, cardio, atendente, secretario)
POST /api/prescriptions (valid payload) returns validation_code starting with GOVBR-
GET /api/dashboard/secretario has kpis (total_patients, absenteeism_rate, adherence_rate, nps, total_prescriptions, exams_pending), by_specialty, med_demand, unit_ranking, weekly_trend(7)

failed.issue: Prisma validation errors are UNCAUGHT and crash the Node process (exit). Supervisor restarts it but concurrent inflight requests receive 502. No global express error middleware catching PrismaClientValidationError to return 400/422.
failed.evidence: backend.err.log: 'PrismaClientValidationError: Invalid prisma.prescription.create() invocation: Argument durationDays is missing.' followed by process exit; 502 returned for subsequent /api/exams, /api/users, /api/dashboard/secretario during restart window.

failed.issue: POST /api/exams with payload {patient_id, exam_type, sigtap_code, cid} returned 502 (process crashed). Endpoint likely missing required Prisma field (e.g. tussCode or examCode).
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/exams -> HTTP 502; log shows PrismaClientValidationError.

failed.issue: POST /api/users as admin with {email,password,name,role=medico,crm,specialty,unit} returned 502 (crash). Missing/mistyped required field on Prisma User model.
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/users -> HTTP 502 after 200 OK login as admin.

failed.issue: GET /api/audit-logs returns empty array despite multiple audited actions (login, prescription create, appointment updates). Either not being written or query filter wrong.
failed.evidence: curl /api/audit-logs -> n=0; but /auth/login and POST /prescriptions succeeded during test.

action_items: Add global Express error handler in server.js that catches PrismaClientValidationError/PrismaClientKnownRequestError and returns 400/422 JSON instead of throwing (prevents process crash).
Fix POST /api/exams handler: inspect required schema fields on Exam model and ensure request body maps to all required Prisma fields (log the exact Prisma validation error to identify missing field).
Fix POST /api/users handler similarly (likely missing a required scalar field on User model, e.g. passwordHash mapping or role enum).
Investigate why AuditLog table is empty: verify audit() helper in auth.js is being awaited and written to DB; confirm GET /api/audit-logs isn't filtering everything out.
Optional: normalize prescription payload to accept both 'duration' string and 'duration_days' int for backward compatibility.

success_rate.frontend: not tested via UI in this iteration
